meta.title: Sergio Delgado — Fullstack Engineer
meta.description: Portfolio of Sergio Delgado. I build SaaS platforms and Web3 applications end to end.

hero.subtitle: I build scalable SaaS platforms and Web3 applications from end to end. Specialized in React, Next.js, NestJS and multi-tenant architectures.
hero.systemReady: READY TO BUILD THE NEXT IDEA.

zelterm.name: Zelterm ERP
zelterm.summary: Multi-tenant ERP for tech-repair SMBs: repair orders, multi-currency POS, inventory with traceability and SUNAT e-invoicing.
bullets: End-to-end repair orders with traceability (protection against claims).
Multi-payment, multi-currency POS (PEN/USD) with SUNAT e-invoicing.
Multi-tenant architecture on a monorepo (Next.js + NestJS + Prisma).
zelterm.overview: Zelterm was born for small tech-repair businesses that had no affordable system — existing options were too expensive. It's inspired by my time selling electronic components. The platform brings order — logging every repair, how the product arrived and what was done — with traceability that also protects them against potential claims. It replaces notebooks and spreadsheets with a single tool: service orders, point of sale, inventory with serial-number tracking and SUNAT e-invoicing.

decisions.title: Multi-tenant from a single database
decisions.body: I started with one shared database to launch fast and cheap. The scaling plan is a business table with a database_id that routes each business to its own database once volume justifies it — a gradual evolution instead of day-one over-engineering.

decisions.title: Multi-currency and multi-payment across the system
decisions.body: The system is configured in PEN or USD, but once the first invoice is issued the currency locks for accounting consistency. Propagating that rule across sales, inventory and reports was the biggest challenge: each flow could introduce conversion bugs.

decisions.title: SUNAT e-invoicing outsourced (Nubefact)
decisions.body: Instead of building the fiscal connector from scratch, I integrated an authorized provider. The complexity boils down to sending the right data, meeting Peruvian regulations with far less risk.

decisions.title: Managed hosting now, VPS later
decisions.body: I chose Vercel + Railway to deploy fast and validate without infrastructure friction. As the client base grows I'll move to a VPS for cost — a decision I make when demand justifies it, not before.

decisions.title: Agent-assisted development (Claude Code + MCP)
decisions.body: I set up specialized agents —frontend, backend, verification, audit and testing— with skills that encode how I would work. Even a skill that challenges whether a feature is really needed, so the software scales gradually without piling on unnecessary complexity from the start.

challenges: Propagating the multi-currency logic across the whole system caused conversion bugs I fixed flow by flow until they were consistent.
I hardened security and sales handling as I better understood the real business cases.
I prioritized finishing it well over fast: I only replace something when I learn a more efficient way than what I already have.

aini28.name: Aini28
aini28.summary: Online plush-toy catalog with a cart whose orders are sent via WhatsApp or email. Managed from Sanity CMS and optimized for cost, SEO and security.
aini28.overview: Plush-toy store for a Peruvian small business. The client needed to showcase the catalog without a payment gateway: orders are assembled in a cart and closed over WhatsApp or email, exactly how he wanted to sell. It has been in production for ~2 years. I did everything end to end —design, frontend, backend and security— with the catalog, offers and checkout fully managed from Sanity.

decisions.title: Headless Sanity for cost
decisions.body: I chose headless Sanity over Shopify/WooCommerce to remove monthly fees: the client only pays for the domain (once a year) and everything else runs on free tiers. From a single panel he manages catalog, offers, cart and checkout.

decisions.title: Orders via WhatsApp/email, no gateway
decisions.body: The client preferred closing sales over WhatsApp. The cart assembles the order and sends it via WhatsApp or email (React Email + Nodemailer), which also covers order confirmation, contact and the complaints book. No gateway: simpler and aligned with how he sells.

decisions.title: Preventive security
decisions.body: Even for a small site I hardened production: CSP, rate limiting with Upstash Redis on the mail endpoints and JSON-LD sanitization. A preventive measure to avoid spam or abuse down the line.

decisions.title: Cost-conscious decisions
decisions.body: I prioritized zero recurring cost down to the details: I use the native tag instead of Next's Image component (whose optimization has a cost) and rely on agents and skills to speed up maintenance.

challenges: No new technical challenges: I already had experience, so the value was in the judgment to pick the right tools.
The focus was minimizing recurring cost: free tiers and native instead of Next's paid component.

catalystbet.name: CatalystBet (Web3)
catalystbet.summary: Decentralized betting dApp with smart contracts, wallets and leaderboard.
catalystbet.overview: Decentralized betting application on testnet with direct smart-contract interaction: phase-based system, wallet connection, on-chain transaction execution and leaderboard.

decisions.title: Direct contract interaction
decisions.body: I connected the frontend directly to the testnet smart contracts to execute bets and claims without an intermediary backend.

decisions.title: Phase-based logic (betting, race, claim)
decisions.body: I modeled the game in phases to clearly reflect an on-chain race cycle and its state.

decisions.title: Web3 UX
decisions.body: I built history, leaderboard and multiple views so the decentralized experience feels clear to the user.

challenges: Syncing frontend state with the on-chain phases (betting, race, claim) without an intermediary backend.
Making the Web3 experience —wallets and transactions— clear for non-technical users.

inuba.summary: Healthcare SaaS platform used by pharmacies for clinical patient management.
bullets: Production system used by 3-4 pharmacies with active professionals.
Patient, treatment, questionnaire and dashboard modules in a team of 4.
Automated testing with Playwright and AI agents.

gato.summary: Web applications focused on performance and user experience.
bullets: Led an accounting dashboard with server-side rendering.
Translated business requirements into functional interfaces.
Built APIs and business logic for the frontend.

ethglobal.title: ETHGlobal Buenos Aires 2025
ethglobal.summary: Project: Hack the World — 3 prizes in the MATE Metaprotocol (EVVM) track.

about.label: ABOUT ME
about.p1: Fullstack developer with 3+ years of experience building SaaS products from scratch and evolving systems in production.
about.p2: I'm passionate about solving complex problems and turning ideas into real products people use — integrating frontend, backend and AI-assisted workflows.
about.quote: I don't just write code. I build systems that scale.
